Every card that touches halting uses a binary gate or a hard cap; none surfaces a mechanism for estimating gap-fillability before committing another wave.
halo_blind_spots.suggested_angle: Hunt for work on gap-type classification — distinguishing structural absences (no source exists) from retrieval failures (source exists, not yet reached). Triangulation-convergence rate across waves is one proxy; look for others.

halo_blind_spots.blind_spot: Zero cards cover cross-wave state accumulation — how the system tracks what each prior wave attempted so the gap-to-lead conversion does not re-dispatch the same angle under a new label. halo_blind_spots.suggested_angle: Look for implementations where the gap-analyzer receives a diff of what was tried, not just what was found — wave-level provenance attached to each card so the next dispatcher can exclude already-explored angles explicitly.

halo_blind_spots.blind_spot: No card examines what happens when the marginal-value wave-sizer and the coverage scorer disagree — the wave-sizer says diminishing returns, the scorer says gaps remain open. halo_blind_spots.suggested_angle: Search for multi-signal termination logic where a resource-cost signal and a coverage-completeness signal are jointly weighted, with an explicit tie-breaking rule when they point in opposite directions.
